title: Sphaeralcea laciniata
intro: Sphaeralcea laciniata, also known as orange globe mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and deeply cut, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Sphaeralcea laciniata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Orange globe mallow is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications like salves and creams, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies for various health concerns.
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea laciniata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Sphaeralcea, which comprises a group of plants commonly known as globe mallow. The Malvaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as mallow family. Sphaeralcea laciniata is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is also known as cut-leaf globe mallow due to its distinctive leaf shape.
